channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Habila’s death: Ohanaeze backs probe, rejects calls for Umahi’s resignation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ohanaeze Ndigbo supports the probe into Mary Habila’s death, urging calm and rejecting calls for Minister Umahi’s resignation until investigations conclude
Read More: https://punchng.com/habilas-death-ohanaeze-backs-probe-rejects-calls-for-umahis-resignation/

item.title: FULL LIST: US warns citizens against travel to 23 countries
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The US State Department has issued a Level 4 “Do Not Travel” advisory for 23 countries, including 11 in Africa. Find out which nations are on the US travel

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Commissioner of Police, Borno State Command, CP Naziru Abdulmajid, has warned armed security personnel against indiscriminate gunfire during weddings, cultural festivities, political gatherings and other celebratory events in the state. The post Borno CP warns against indiscriminate gunfire at w

item.title: Federal Govt, UNDP Partner To Cut Borrowing Costs
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government, in partnership with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), has begun efforts to improve Nigeria’s sovereign credit ratings as part of a broader strategy to reduce the country’s borrowing costs and attract more investment. Speaking at the debriefing meeting on the Cr

item.title: Police launch manhunt for teacher who allegedly flogged student to death
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “Photographs of the deceased were taken, and preliminary observations revealed visible marks of violence on different parts of the victim’s body,” he said. The post Police launch manhunt for teacher who allegedly flogged student to death appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: Stakeholders Champion IP-backed Financing Framework For Digital, Creative Sectors
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Public and private sector stakeholders in Nigeria are accelerating efforts to build a framework that would allow businesses in the country’s digital and creative economy to use intellectual property (IP) assets as collateral for securing financing; a move designed to broaden capital access for innov

item.title: Best finishers, defensive kings & runners - World Cup finalists in numbers
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: European champions Spain will face World Cup holders and South American champions Argentina in the final. How do they compare? BBC Sport takes a look at the numbers.

item.title: Stocks End Week Lower As Blue-chip Losses Pull Market Cap Down By N612bn
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian equities market closed the week in negative territory as heavy losses in BUA Cement Plc and 34 others weighed on market performance. The All-Share Index dipped 0.14 per cent week-on-week to close at 243,462.13 points, while market capitalization lost N612 billion to close the week at N

item.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Iran has retaliated against US allies in the region, with Kuwait reporting that a power and water plant had been hit.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) has warned newly awarded Petroleum Prospecting Licence (PPL) holders to develop their assets or face forfeiture under the Petroleum Industry Act’s “drill or drop” provision. The NUPRC chief executive, Oritsemeyiwa Eyesan, speaking during

item.title: Gombe Security Chiefs warn against election violence
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Security agencies in Gombe State have warned political parties against engaging in violence, early campaigns and other actions capable of undermining peace ahead of the 2027 general elections. item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Wayne Rooney rows down the Hudson River with fellow BBC pundits Joe Hart and Micah Richards.

item.description: A divorce-seeking woman, Doris Omega, has urged an Upper Customary Court in Kafanchan, Kaduna State, to dissolve her marriage and order her husband, Daniel, to pay her N200,000 in damages. According to the News Agency of Nigeria (NAN), Doris told the court that her husband had lost interest in her a

item.title: Customs Reforms Eliminate Multiple Alerts, Enhance Trade Efficiency At PTML
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS), Port and Terminal Multiservices Limited (PTML) Command, said it has intensified trade facilitation measures by promoting 24-hour cargo clearance and eliminating multiple alerts that previously slowed cargo processing. The officer in charge of the Trade Facilitation

item.title: Iran’s Guards say two oil tankers explode after hitting mines in Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said Saturday that two oil tankers transiting through the Strait of Hormuz hit mines and exploded, a claim disputed by the US military. The post Iran’s Guards say two oil tankers explode after hitting mines in Hormuz appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Opta supercomputer has tipped Spain to beat reigning champion Argentina in the final of the World Cup 2026 on Sunday, TNT Sports reports. Ahead of the epic encounter, the supercomputer has given Spain a 59% chance of lifting the trophy in New Jersey on Sunday, while Argentina have a 41% chance of gl

The suspects were arrested on Friday, 17th July during a security operation led by the Deputy Governor of Nasarawa S

item.title: Olojo Kosoko Ruling House alleges police intimidation amid Lagos chieftaincy dispute
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Olojo Kosoko Ruling House of Lagos State has accused unnamed individuals of using police officers to intimidate members of the family and impose what it described as an unlawful siege on the Oloja of Lagos Palace, despite a subsisting court order directing all parties to maintain the status quo.

item.title: Despite her childlessness, my wife hauls insults at me, pastor tells court
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A clergyman, Enoch, has sought the dissolution of his three-year-old union with his wife, Omolabake, whom he stated was getting on his nerves. Enoch explained that Omolabake was as gentle as a dove when they met, but that she suddenly changed a few years into their marriage. Enoch said that Omolabak

item.title: Fine, electric mountain bikes don’t suck
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Cheater, I'd grumble between huffs as yet another e-bike rider casually skittered past me on a steep ascent. It's this purist attitude that, for years, has left me blind to one simple fact: electric mountain bikes are fun! My attitude adjustment came a few weeks ago, the very first time I rode an Am

item.description: A "blockbuster fight" between Anthony Joshua and Moses Itauma in Nigeria would create more global interest than Muhammad Ali’s 'Rumble in the Jungle', says promoter Dr Ezekiel Adamu.

item.title: Breaking: Cyberattack disrupts NBA presidential election
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Electoral Committee of the Nigerian Bar Association, ECNBA, has announced an indefinite delay in the commencement of voting for the 2026 NBA National Elections following what it described as a coordinated cyberattack on its electronic voting platform. item.title: Butter and my health
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: I love eating butter a lot. Kindly let me know if there is any way this can adversely affect my health. Promise (by SMS) Daily consumption of butter can have both positive and negative effects on health. While butter is a source of fatsoluble vitamins and other nutrients, it is also high in calories

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: I recently noticed bright red blood in my stool after defaecation. Kindly advise me on this. Jerom (by SMS) Bright red blood in stool means there’s bleeding somewhere in your digestive tract very close to your anus. The difference between seeing a bright red blood and a dark red one is that the brig

item.title: ‘I am concerned,’ Scaloni on Argentina’s Spain fear before World Cup final
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “If Spain leaves the hotel, I am already concerned. Once they are on the bus and they leave the hotel, I am concerned. They are a great team. Everything concerns me about Spain,” he said.

item.title: WAFCON 2026: Please Don’t Beat Us 5-0, 1-0 Is Enough —Malawi Captain Pleads With Super Falcons
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Malawi captain, Tabitha Chawinga, has jokingly pleaded with the Super Falcons not to inflict a heavy defeat on her team when the two sides clash in their opening Group C game at the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations in Rabat, Morocco. The Super Falcons have been drawn alongside Malawi, Zambia and E

item.title: I can’t marry a man who earns less than me, says BBNaija star Sabrina
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former Big Brother Naija 10/10 housemate, Sabrina Idukpaye, has said income level would play a major role in her choice of a life partner. The post I can’t marry a man who earns less than me, says BBNaija star Sabrina appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Kolkata sings for Messi as World Cup fever takes hold
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: South Asia’s greatest football city has united behind its footballing saint.

item.title: France vs England: Deschamps set for final World Cup match as Zidane waits
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Deschamps, who led France to the 2018 World Cup title, bids farewell after Saturday's third-place playoff with England.

item.title: Orban Set For Loan Move To Turkish Club
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria striker Gift Orban is on the verge of joining newly promoted Turkish Süper Lig club Amedspor on loan for the 2026/27 season, Completesports.com reports. According to reports from Turkey, Amedspor have reached an agreement with Bundesliga outfit TSG Hoffenheim and the player over a season-lon

item.title: Saturday Spontaneous Post
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: #TodayisforRestandRadiance #positivesaturday #weekendglow #restandrecharge #purehappiness #calmjoy #saturdayeaseGood Morning. Today, please rest your mind and renew your spirit because God refreshes those who pause and breathe in His peace.“He restores my soul.” — Psalm 23:3❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️

item.title: No going back on July 31 recapitalisation deadline, NAICOM warns insurers
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Omosehin assured stakeholders that the commission would ensure a transparent, fair and firm verification process, stressing that every operator must demonstrate financial soundness, regulatory compliance and operational readiness.

item.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.

item.title: Disappointed France and England to face off in third place World Cup match
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: France and England meet for their final World Cup match on Saturday competing for third place after failing to reach Sunday's final. Neither of the disappointed teams seems to relish the prospect of the clash: "The best thing for France and England would be for this match not to exist," said France

item.title: 2027: Why ADC Supreme Court battle may weaken anti-APC coalition
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By the time the Supreme Court finally settles the battle over the African Democratic Congress (ADC), the legal questions may have been answered The post 2027: Why ADC Supreme Court battle may weaken anti-APC coalition appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 41
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: World Cup 2026: Do supporters know the World Cup records?
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Which is the only team to have competed in every World Cup? And which has won the most? Throughout the tournament, we asked fans about World Cup records.

item.title: Two Anambra men bag death sentence for rape, murder of 17-yr-old
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: While delivering his judgement on Friday the presiding judge, Justice U. I. Ndigwe held that the prosecution had proved the three counts charges against the defendants beyond reasonable doubt.

item.title: Ukraine drone strike kills seven in Russian attack
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “Seven night-shift employees were killed when enemy UAVs hit a Wildberries logistics centre,” Pervyshov said, adding that 24 people were injured in the strike.

item.title: Rot in constituency projects
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The latest revelations on the abuse of constituency projects have exposed, in painful detail, how Nigeria’s budgeting process has been turned into a bazaar where public institutions are stripped of their mandates and transformed into conduits for political patronage. It is a system that has held gov

item.title: ‘Why AKK project is being delayed’
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: In this interview, Dr Bala Zakka, a petroleum engineer, energy consultant, chartered accountant, tax expert and public affairs analyst spoke on how Nigeria is losing revenue from the delay in completing the Ajaokuta-Kaduna-Kano (AKK) gas pipeline.   Six years after flag-off, the Ajaokuta-Kaduna

item.title: Reimagining Nigerian media in an era of distrust
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: It’s no longer news that some public institutions in Nigeria don’t work for the common man. Rather, they work for the elites. This is evident in nearly every public sector. As a nation, Nigeria has failed its citizens. From one corruption scandal to the next, from embezzlement to outright neglect, c

item.title: 2026 National Assembly Open Day: Matters arising
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Tenth National Assembly (2023-2027) held its three-day 2026 Open Day activities between July 14 and 16, 2026, which offered the general public an opportunity to see aspects of the routine enterprise of Nigeria’s central legislature. With the theme: “Three Years of the 10th Assembly: Advancing Tr
